finding: Direct structural YAML mapping (bsv_typedef field) compiles but provides no abstraction benefit
Template-based compiler produces correct output for simple typedefs but doesn't leverage LLM capabilities
YAML is 24% larger than original BSV - defeats purpose of information density
Declarative YAML descriptions + LLM generation produces functionally correct BSV for pure functions
Natural language descriptions are 3.75x more verbose than the BSV code they describe - ID metric needs optimization
Language independence can be achieved by scoping language-specific info to knowledge.<lang> while keeping structural YAML fields generic
LLM correctly handles BSV-specific constructs (msb(), truncate(), zeroExtend(), bit concatenation) from declarative descriptions alone
Cross-file function dependencies must be explicitly resolved in YAML - calcPktNumByLength from Utils4Test.bsv caused compilation failure
Generated BSV (1738 bytes) is larger than original (982 bytes) due to formatting and comments added by LLM
